title: cleomella multicaulis
intro: Cleomella multicaulis, also known as Texas honeyvine, is a perennial climber native to North America, belonging to the Cleomaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Cleomella multicaulis include its ability to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and aid in respiratory problems. Cleomella multicaulis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for various ailments.
